source: Pre-trained Language Model based Ranking in Baidu Search

concepts.finding: Baidu deploys pre-trained language model (ERNIE) for search ranking in production. Two-stage: (1) candidate generation via embedding retrieval, (2) ranking with cross-attention PLM. Key challenges: latency (must respond in <50ms), training efficiency (500B+ samples), cold-start queries. Knowledge-enhanced pre-training improves ranking for entity-centric queries.
concepts.implication: Production keyword-intent systems face latency constraints. Two-stage architecture: fast embedding retrieval (candidate intent generation) followed by precise PLM re-ranking (intent disambiguation). Knowledge-enhanced models help for entity-related keywords.
concepts.open_question: Is a two-stage keyword-intent system (fast candidate generation + PLM re-ranking) practical for real-time intent prediction? What latency budget is feasible?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A two-stage keyword-intent system (embedding retrieval + PLM re-ranker) achieves 90%+ accuracy of pure PLM with <10ms latency at production scale.
